mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of rising rate instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC > 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market contraction that reduces aggregate corporate revenues, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, which increases near-term corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) or direction flips to negative in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side momentum that reduces input cost pressures and boosts short-term equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero (Rank IC < 0.02) during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.5 percent forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy stances, which suppresses equity risk premiums and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of high-inflation observations.

mechanism_narrative: Divergent momentum where total nonfarm payrolls increase while the unemployment rate declines confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline by > 5% Sharpe ratio over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above trend establishes a baseline of expanding economic output that reduces default risk and supports immediate equity price appreciation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDP growth and 5-day returns remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signals immediate supply chain bottlenecks and weakening manufacturing activity, which compresses profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20d returns in > 55% of contraction instances despite the signal.
